steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to specify oxygen's total mass relative to all elements on Earth. A mass fraction is defined in the notes as that exact ratio, and oxygen's mass fraction is 297,000 ppm. The correct choice must state that value.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Oxygen's abundance...is greater than vanadium's...’: compares oxygen to vanadium rather than to all elements; doesn't specify oxygen's mass fraction value. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Oxygen's abundance can be expressed by a mass fraction...’: defines the concept but omits the specific 297,000 ppm value. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The mass fraction of oxygen on Earth is 297,000 ppm.’: states the exact mass fraction, which by definition is the ratio of oxygen's mass to the combined mass of all elements. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Oxygen's mass...is 105 ppm.’: uses vanadium's value (105 ppm) for oxygen — factually wrong. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
